summary: narrow follow-up fix completed in group_session.py: gate authority no longer falls back to client-declared author_role for unknown senders, and post-advance gate snapshot top-level fields now immediately reflect the newly advanced stage while retaining old-stage snapshots and advanced_from/advanced_to markers. minimal local validation used a stub-backed import plus py_compile, confirmed an intruder claiming manager cannot satisfy manager_done or advance the stage, confirmed a registered manager still advances normally, and confirmed after a valid step1 -> step2 advance that GroupSession.current_stage matches gate_snapshot.current_stage in the same turn.
tests: finding1 stub validation: unknown sender claiming author_role=manager does not satisfy manager_done and does not advance current_stage; registered manager still advances to step2
finding2 stub validation: after valid advance, GroupSession.current_stage == gate_snapshot.current_stage in same turn, stage_snapshots retains step1, advanced_from/advanced_to preserved
